# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

We are committed to providing a welcoming and inspiring community for all. We expect all participants in our community to uphold these principles and help us maintain a respectful environment.

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to creating a positive environment include:

- Being respectful and inclusive of different viewpoints and experiences
- Providing and gracefully accepting constructive criticism
- Focusing on what is best for the community
- Showing empathy towards other community members

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

- Harassment, intimidation, or discrimination of any kind
- Personal attacks or insulting comments
- Trolling, spamming, or deliberately disruptive behavior
- Publishing private information without consent
- Any other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting

## Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported by contacting the project maintainers. All reports will be reviewed and investigated promptly.

All community leaders are obligated to respect the privacy and security of the reporter of any incident.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all community spaces, including but not limited to:

- GitHub issues and pull requests
- Project discussions and documentation
- Community chat or forums
- Any other spaces representing the project or community

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/), version 2.0, available at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2_0/code_of_conduct.html.
